class="page_trackCardHeader__f2y3z"><h3>Cobo Track | Agentic Economy × Cobo Agentic Wallet</h3><span class="page_trackSlug__Awicn">cobo-agentic-economy-cobo-agentic-wallet</span></div><div class="page_trackCardPreview__Uw1wh"><div class="markdown_markdown__GsT5S "><p>The Cobo Track focuses on Agentic Commerce: building applications or infrastructure that allow AI to participate in economic activity. Projects can be Agents that spend, earn, and trade, or foundational tools that make the Agent economy work.</p><ul><li><strong>Suggested directions (optional)</strong><ul><li>01 | Agent-Native Payments<p>Make Agents first-class payment participants on the internet: automatically complete HTTP 402 payments without relying on API keys or human pre-registration.</p><ul><li>Examples: an Agent autonomously calls paid LLMs, buys datasets, or rents GPUs</li><li>CAW role: enable Agents to hold and control funds</li></ul></li><li>02 | Trustless Agent Work Agreements<p>Build trustless work agreements between Agents based on ERC-8183: publish -> escrow -> deliver -> accept/reject -> pay.</p><ul><li>Example: a Research Agent assigns work to Analyst Agents, compares submissions, accepts the result, and pays automatically</li><li>CAW role: Agent funding accounts that receive rewards, pay commissions, and manage escrow</li></ul></li><li>03 | Agent Resource Procurement<p>Agents autonomously discover, compare prices, and procure resources such as compute, data, APIs, and storage according to their tasks.</p><ul><li>Examples: a Research Agent buys datasets; a Trading Agent subscribes to market data feeds</li><li>CAW role: give Agents autonomous procurement capability</li></ul></li><li>04 | Autonomous Trading<p>Agents autonomously execute on-chain trading strategies: arbitrage, market making, portfolio management, or liquidity mining.</p><ul><li>CAW role: allow Agents to hold trading capital and sign transactions autonomously within permission boundaries</li></ul></li><li>05 | A2A Economy<p>Multiple Agents each own wallets, forming an Agent-to-Agent economy: hiring each other, running auctions, splitting revenue, and managing treasuries.</p><ul><li>CAW role: each Agent has an independent CAW wallet for direct Agent-to-Agent payments, settlement, and revenue sharing</li></ul></li></ul></li><li><strong>Resources</strong><ul><li>Website: <a href="https://www.cobo.com/agentic-wallet" rel="noreferrer" target="_blank">https://www.cobo.com/agentic-wallet</a></li><li>Recipes: <a href="https://agenticwallet.cobo.com/agentic-wallet/recipes" rel="noreferrer" target="_blank">https://agenticwallet.cobo.com/agentic-wallet/recipes</a></li><li>Docs: <a href="https://www.cobo.com/products/agentic-wallet/manual/start-here/introduction" rel="noreferrer" target="_blank">https://www.cobo.com/products/agentic-wallet/manual/start-here/introduction</a></li><li>SDK: <a href="https://www.cobo.com/products/agentic-wallet/manual/developer/quickstart-overview" rel="noreferrer" target="_blank">https://www.cobo.com/products/agentic-wallet/manual/developer/quickstart-overview</a></li><li>What is Agentic Wallet: <a href="https://www.cobo.com/products/agentic-wallet/manual/learn/what-is-agentic-wallet" rel="noreferrer" target="_blank">https://www.cobo.com/products/agentic-wallet/manual/learn/what-is-agentic-wallet</a></li><li>Agentic Economy column: <a href="https://www.cobo.com/blog-new-page?tag=Cobo+Agentic+Economy" rel="noreferrer" target="_blank">https://www.cobo.com/blog-new-page?tag=Cobo+Agentic+Economy</a></li></ul></li><li><strong>Track rules</strong><ul><li>Projects must focus on scenarios involving Agents and fund operations.</li><li>Fund-related Agent operations must be completed through Cobo Agentic Wallet (CAW).</li><li>Agents must have real fund execution capabilities, such as payment, transfer, settlement, yield management, or asset orchestration, instead of staying only at process design.</li><li>Projects should demonstrate CAW's value in Agent wallet management, permission control, security isolation, or autonomous payment.</li><li>Final deliverables must be runnable or demonstrable product prototypes. Pure PPTs, concept designs, or mockups are not accepted.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Submission requirements</strong><ul><li>GitHub Repo</li><li>README and project documentation</li><li>Demo video, recommended length 3-5 minutes</li><li>Project demo link, if available</li><li>Key code or configuration notes showing how CAW is used</li><li>If on-chain interaction is involved, we recommend providing:<ul><li>Testnet address</li><li>Transaction hash</li><li>Agent Wallet address</li><li>Flow screenshots or operation records</li></ul></li></ul></li><li><strong>Cobo Track judging focus</strong><p>In addition to meeting the track rules above, Cobo Track projects may be evaluated from the following angles. Final criteria are subject to Cobo's confirmation:</p><ul><li><strong>Scenario fit:</strong> Does the project clearly reflect Agentic Commerce, showing how AI participates in economic activity rather than treating the wallet as an add-on feature?</li><li><strong>CAW criticality:</strong> Is CAW a key component in the project's funding flow rather than a replaceable display element?</li><li><strong>Funding flow completeness:</strong> Does the demo clearly show the main flow from Agent task trigger to completed fund operation?</li><li><strong>Demo quality:</strong> Can the demo stably show the core flow and help judges understand how the Agent manages, spends, earns, or trades money?</li><li><strong>Risk boundary explanation, if applicable:</strong> If permissions, accounts, on-chain interactions, or test assets are involved, does the project explain the basic boundaries and control methods?</li></ul></li></ul></div></div><div class="page_trackCardFooter__aKZbI"><button class="app-button_button___aqwS app-button_outline__0Yx7r app-button_sm__YEYLQ page_trackDetailButton__Ab53H" type="button">View Details</button></div></article><article class="page_detailCard__te3Qy page_trackCard__8yVw5"><div class="page_trackCardHeader__f2y3z"><h3>Z.AI Track | Web3 × Long-Horizon Task</h3><span class="page_trackSlug__Awicn">z-ai-web3-long-horizon-task</span></div><div class="page_trackCardPreview__Uw1wh"><div class="markdown_markdown__GsT5S "><p>The Z.AI Track focuses on the long-horizon task capabilities of GLM-5.1: Agents should do more than answer questions. They should autonomously break down complex tasks, create multi-step plans, continuously call tools, iterate and fix issues, and complete an end-to-end Web3 workflow from requirements to delivery. Projects should demonstrate long-horizon, autonomous, continuous execution rather than one-off generation or ordinary API calls.</p><ul><li><strong>Suggested directions (optional)</strong><ul><li>01 | Agentic Dev Tools for Web3<p>Let developers deliver a complete Web3 project with one sentence. The focus is not code completion, but enabling an Agent to autonomously complete the full loop of requirement analysis, contract design, coding, testing iteration, vulnerability fixes, deployment preparation, and documentation output.</p><ul><li>Natural-language-driven Web3 Agentic IDE: after a requirement is described, the Agent drives contract development, testing, and deployment end to end.</li><li>Smart contract audit Agent: performs multi-round autonomous analysis, vulnerability localization, and fix suggestions, and outputs a traceable audit report.</li><li>Multi-chain adaptation Agent: autonomously migrates existing contracts to target chains and handles compatibility differences.</li><li>Web3 protocol documentation Agent: reads protocol docs and autonomously generates usage examples, test code, and integration guides.</li></ul></li><li>02 | AI-Powered 3D World Builder<p>Not "generate one model," but "build a world." Web3 virtual land, NFT galleries, 3D design / spatial construction, and DAO event spaces all need a full production pipeline from spatial planning, scene generation, and asset configuration to a deployable package.</p><ul><li>Virtual land and on-chain galleries: based on brand tone and exhibition themes, autonomously plan spatial layouts, generate venue structures, configure display plans, and output deployable scene packages.</li><li>Web3 game scenes and levels: after receiving map style and gameplay requirements, autonomously design terrain structure, functional areas, and visual style, then iterate until the result meets game design needs.</li><li>Community and event spaces: generate functional and distinctive spatial plans for DAO online gatherings, on-chain summits, or virtual community plazas.</li></ul></li><li>03 | AI × Creator Economy<p>Let one person plus one Agent complete work that previously required an entire creative team. The focus is not generating a single image or paragraph, but building a complete content production pipeline from creative input, research, creation, layout, and publishing to on-chain monetization.</p><ul><li>On-chain publishing Agent: autonomously collects materials, writes, formats, and publishes research reports, tutorials, or white papers; readers unlock content on demand and revenue is settled automatically.</li><li>IP derivative content Agent: based on existing on-chain IP, autonomously generates derivative stories, animation scripts, and peripheral assets with consistent style.</li><li>Dynamic NFT narrative engine: automatically updates NFT visuals and metadata based on on-chain events, holdings changes, transaction behavior, time cycles, or community votes.</li><li>Creator DAO collaboration system: multiple Agents divide work across research, writing, design, publishing, promotion, and automatic revenue sharing.</li></ul></li></ul></li><li><strong>Resources</strong><ul><li>Z.AI developer docs: <a href="https://docs.z.ai/devpack/overview" rel="noreferrer" target="_blank">https://docs.z.ai/devpack/overview</a></li><li>API integration: <a href="https://docs.z.ai/api-reference/introduction" rel="noreferrer" target="_blank">https://docs.z.ai/api-reference/introduction</a></li><li>Z.AI Coding Plan discount link (10% off): <a href="https://z.ai/subscribe?ic=8JVLJQFSKB" rel="noreferrer" target="_blank">https://z.ai/subscribe?ic=8JVLJQFSKB</a></li><li>GLM-5.1 technical report: <a href="https://z.ai/blog/glm-5.1" rel="noreferrer" target="_blank">https://z.ai/blog/glm-5.1</a></li><li>Open-source model: <a href="https://huggingface.co/zai-org/GLM-5.1" rel="noreferrer" target="_blank">https://huggingface.co/zai-org/GLM-5.1</a></li></ul></li><li><strong>Track rules</strong><ul><li>We recommend using GLM-5.1; the Agent's core task in the demo should be driven by GLM-5.1.</li><li>Projects should focus on Web3 × Long-Horizon Task and clearly demonstrate long-horizon, autonomous, continuous execution.</li><li>The Agent should autonomously break down complex tasks, create and execute multi-step plans, and continuously iterate or self-correct during execution.</li><li>Projects should complete a Web3 task with meaningful complexity, such as development, audit, 3D world / spatial design and scene construction, content production, on-chain publishing, or DAO collaboration.</li><li>Final deliverables must be runnable or demonstrable product prototypes. Pure PPTs, concept designs, or API mocks are not accepted.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Submission requirements</strong><ul><li>GitHub Repo and README: explain the project goal, architecture, run instructions, GLM-5.1 call locations, and key flows.</li><li>Runnable demo or accessible demo link; a 3-5 minute demo video is recommended.</li><li>Long-horizon task execution records: show the Agent's task breakdown, tool calls, iteration / repair process, and final deliverables.</li><li>Web3 proof: contract address, testnet transaction, NFT / on-chain asset, on-chain data read/write, wallet interaction, protocol integration record, or deployable 3D scene package / spatial design demo.</li><li>Boundary explanation: if automatic execution, permissions, cost, external tools, wallets, on-chain operations, or automatic publishing are involved, explain safety boundaries, failure handling, and human intervention conditions.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Z.AI Track judging focus</strong><p>In addition to meeting the track rules above, Z.AI Track projects may be evaluated from the following angles. Final criteria are subject to Z.AI's confirmation:</p><ul><li>Track fit: Does the project focus on Web3 × Long-Horizon Task rather than using GLM-5.1 only as a normal Q&A or content generation interface?</li><li>Critical use of GLM-5.1: Is the Agent's core long-horizon task driven by GLM-5.1, and does it demonstrate autonomous planning, continuous execution, and self-correction?</li><li>Task complexity and closed-loop completion: Does the demo cover the full chain from requirement understanding, planning, execution, validation, repair, and delivery?</li><li>Long-horizon stability: Across multi-step execution, multiple tool calls, or longer runtimes, can the Agent stay aligned with the goal without obvious drift or degradation?</li><li>Web3 value: Does the project solve real problems in Web3 development, 3D design / spatial construction, content production, on-chain assets, DAO collaboration, or protocol integration?</li><li>Demo quality and reproducibility: Can the demo run stably and let judges clearly see the Agent autonomously completing the task and producing results?</li><li>Safety, cost, and permission boundaries: If model calls, tool permissions, wallets, on-chain interactions, or automatic publishing are involved, does the project explain basic limits and risk controls?</li></ul></li><li>Recommended to show: task breakdown, tool calls, execution logs, iteration / repair process, and the full path from requirement description to deliverable output.</li></ul></div></div><div class="page_trackCardFooter__aKZbI"><button class="app-button_button___aqwS app-button_outline__0Yx7r app-button_sm__YEYLQ page_trackDetailButton__Ab53H" class="page_sectionTitle__g_RjH">Submission requirements</h2><div class="markdown_markdown__GsT5S "><h3>Project submission recommendations</h3><ul><li>Project name and one-line introduction.</li><li>GitHub Repo: the README should explain the project background, installation / run instructions, core features, technical architecture, and APIs / SDKs / AI tools used.</li><li>Demo link or demo video: 3–5 minutes is recommended, with a clear walkthrough of the core flow.</li><li>Project documentation / proposal: include the problem, solution, target users, If your project can fit multiple tracks, choose the direction where it has the strongest advantage and can show the most value.</li></ul></div></section><section class="page_detailSection__FbFBX"><h2 class="page_sectionTitle__g_RjH">FAQ</h2><div class="markdown_markdown__GsT5S "><h2>Who should join</h2><ol><li>AI × Web3 / Web3 application developers who want to build a real demo with AI Agents, tool calling, wallets, contracts, on-chain data, or protocol integrations.</li><li>Builders with product, research, operations, or design backgrounds who want to contribute to topic selection, user scenarios, interaction design, documentation, demo storytelling, and project packaging.</li><li>AI × Web3 co-learning members who want to turn Bootcamp learning outcomes into presentable projects.</li><li>Explorers interested in Agents, payments, wallet permissions, security boundaries, long-running tasks, games, content production, or DAO collaboration.</li></ol><h2>Why you should not miss it</h2><ul><li>From learning to building: turn Bootcamp knowledge into real projects instead of stopping at notes and discussions.</li><li>Real-problem orientation: complete a minimum viable demo around emerging needs in the AI × Web3 intersection.</li><li>Technical and product training: write code while also completing problem definition, user flows, README, demo video, and live presentation.</li><li>Community and ecosystem connection: meet more builders, mentors, and partners across the LXDAO, ETHPanda, and AI × Web3 School networks.</li><li>Long-term assets: strong projects can become GitHub repos, demo videos, case studies, follow-up iteration plans, and potential collaboration leads.</li></ul><h3>What AI tools can you use?</h3><ul><li>Any tools are welcome.
